items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.

source.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers
source.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia and their carers.
source.setting: Initial assessment, specialist assessment, ongoing care coordination, and support.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult memory and cognitive concern assessment and support; partial for undifferentiated memory symptoms and not directly validated for UAE referral, driving, or social-care pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.6 and 1.11.1–1.11.8 — driving information and carer support

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.

items.rationale: The exact dementia section supports documenting that driving impact, legal information, carer views, concerns and questions were discussed with a person living with dementia; it does not determine fitness to drive.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
source.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

items.rationale: The exact falls assessment section supports clinician-assessed cognition, dizziness, vision, hearing, mobility, gait, medicines and function when relevant to a driving discussion.
